page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7a.large is a memory-optimized virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 2 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical AMD EPYC 7T83 cores running at a clock speed of 2.55 GHz. The instance is configured with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU capabilities, with stress-ng single-core performance ranking in the top 25 percent. However, multi-core CPU performance and overall Passmark CPU scores fall into the bottom 25 percent, reflecting the low core count. Memory bandwidth benchmarks show weak to poor performance, with uncached read and write speeds ranking in the bottom 25 percent and bottom 10 percent respectively. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks perform at average levels, while database operations and Redis SET throughput are positioned in the bottom 10 percent.

Given its high memory density relative to its compute resources, this instance offers a cost-efficient profile for memory-bound workloads that do not require high parallel processing power or local storage. It is suitable for lightweight database caching, single-threaded data processing, small-scale web serving, and basic text generation tasks using small language models.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7a.large is a memory-optimized virtual server instance built on the KVM hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 2 dedicated vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 7T83 processor running at 2.55 GHz, paired with 16.0 GB of RAM, which provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmark data shows strong single-core CPU performance, though multi-core execution and memory bandwidth are relatively weak. This resource profile makes the instance highly suitable for memory-intensive, low-concurrency workloads such as lightweight caching, single-threaded data processing, and small-scale web serving.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and high memory density for low-concurrency, memory-bound workloads.

16.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
